item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this after accidentally clicking the Random SCP button. It was short, so I decided to read it. And, um… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: forgive if i'm misunderstanding but from how interpret this, the murders are being committed using the concept of the knife that wasn't made in the set.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ironic, was that intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a general note, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

That said, it looks like wikidot's a bit slow on the uptake at the moment for some users, and attempting to click "post" repeatedly after an ajax error will result in multiple posts. If you do see an ajax error or the like, please refresh the page to make sure that the comment didn't already go through before attempting to post it again.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
As a general note, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

That said, it looks like wikidot's a bit slow on the update at the moment and attempting to click "post" repeatedly after an ajax error will result in multiple posts. If you do see an ajax error or the like, please refresh the page to make sure that the comment didn't already go through before attempting to post it again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fellow Fallen Londoner!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fellow Fallen Londoner!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fellow Fallen Londoner!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does it mean that the weapon doesn't exist? How did the Foundation come to that conclusion? How do they explain the murders that have been committed with it, and how do they explain the evidence? Wouldn't it have been simpler and more reasonable for them to just conclude that a non-anomalous copy of the six non-anomalous knives has been used to kill a lot of people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SPOILER
– hide block

There's no twist or revelation here; the article is an exercise in negative space. I'm trying to 1) Present an unsolvable puzzle that 'feels' like it has a solution, 2) Establish a low-key sort of dread/anxiety, and 3) Get the reader into a headspace where they imagine themselves into fear.

Here's the basics:

  • The article describes a set of six non-anomalous knives, all of which have been intermittently misidentified as a single murder weapon used in literally hundreds of homicides over a century.
  • It establishes that these six non-anomalous knives are part of a set that was never complete (the creator was killed before he could finish it).
  • It never elaborates on who's been doing these murders, or why (multiple people? One person?).
  • It establishes that the actual murder weapon, despite all evidence to the contrary, does not exist.

The article might not work for everyone; it was kind of an experiment more than anything. But that's the basic gist of it — it's an article that's more about what's left out than what's put in.

Hence the title: Absence of a Knife.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I'm missing something here. Could someone please explain to me why people find this SCP so appealing?

this is how I understood it
it's really not much

All I'm seeing is that there's either a 7th knife or the murder weapon was never one of the knives in the first place, and just looks like the knives.


Any explanation would be appreciated!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I thought I had it the first time around (these knives where somehow crafted to make everyone think they were being used for some free publicity, except something got screwed at some point and now they make everyone think they kill people), but all the other possibilities appear everytime I think about it and it just gives me a headache.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i have no idea what im afraid of

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet a perfect example of what an SCP should be, most of the time anyways

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this. It tells a scary story through implication rather than explicit description of scary things, and it does so in a short, minimalist fashion. I really enjoyed the implication that these knives have been killing in hundreds of murders for years, and no one can explain how.

I would like to see this expanded a little. Maybe with an example case, so we can see just what exactly is meant by "despite all evidence to the contrary, no actual murder weapon exists". This article is a little shorter than I'd prefer anyway. But that's a fairly minor quibble, and I still really enjoyed it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can’t work out whether I’m missing an implication here, or if this just falls on the wrong side of my personal threshold for “enough story”. No vote at this stage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like my brain has just been molested. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayy, nice to see this idea up on the wiki! I was worried at first that this would run into the potential issue I brought up in chat about the article not having any draw beyond the weird chain of murders, but having the murders still happening without a knife makes this work perfectly for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of Jack-of-Smiles

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disconcerting, chilling, charming. I swear I was getting goose bumps on the last two paragraphs.

I can't help but love how much you've accomplished in so little space. Take your well-deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent piece, as ever- a very good example of using that which is unsaid to tell a story, and done in a pleasantly minimalist fashion. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if the murder weapon can only be seen through gaslight.

In any way, that's an interesting piece. Hard to understand at first, but worth to think about. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. Ummm… I don't know what to say other than that, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One beautiful day in the middle of the night,
Two dead boys got up to fight.

Back to back they faced each other,
Drew their swords and shot each other.

A deaf policeman heard the noise,
Then came and shot those two dead boys.

Now, if you don't believe this lie is true,
Go ask the blind man -- he saw it too.

SOURCES:

  • Image here.
One possible explanation?
– hide block

There's a seventh knife.

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
